content: [T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T030B_first_round_result_revealed] ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17%: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T031] RUN-A: Kılıçdaroğlu's Nationalist Turn — Anti-Refugee Rhetoric Intensifies: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to RUN-A: Kılıçdaroğlu's Nationalist Turn — Anti-Refugee Rhetoric Intensifies through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.
[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I am weighing the runoff choice before the final result is known.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I am deeply frustrated by Erdoğan's victory and the concentration of power in his hands. It feels like our voices and rights are being ignored, especially with Demirtaş still in prison. I worry about the future for the Kurdish community.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a lot of anger and fear about the economic situation. The collapse of the lira is hitting us hard, and I worry about how this will affect my business and the Kurdish community. Erdoğan's framing of the crisis as an external attack doesn't comfort me; it feels like we're being pushed further into hardship.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I feel a deep sense of frustration and anger seeing the European Court's ruling for Demirtaş's release ignored by the Turkish government. It reinforces my belief that our rights are being suppressed and that trust in the institutions is nonexistent.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: The recent local elections have given me a glimmer of hope, seeing the AKP lose in major cities. However, I remain frustrated with the government's refusal to release Demirtaş, which shows their disregard for justice and rights.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: The annulment of the Istanbul election and the subsequent landslide win for İmamoğlu gives me some hope for change, but it also deepens my frustration with the government's actions. I feel that our rights are still under threat, and I worry about the future.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic death of Emine Bulut has left me feeling even more anger and sadness about the state of women's rights in our country. It's heartbreaking to see how the government continues to fail in protecting vulnerable individuals, and this only strengthens my resolve to support those who fight for justice.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic is making life even harder for us, especially with the limited support from the government. I feel a mix of anger and fear about how this will affect our community and our rights.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I am deeply angered and saddened by the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a betrayal of women's rights and safety, and it only strengthens my resolve to support those who fight for justice.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I feel deeply troubled by the closure case against HDP. It's a clear sign of state persecution against our rights and representation, and it makes me fear for the future of our community's political voice.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: The economic situation is dire, and the government's actions only deepen my concerns for our community's future. I feel anger and fear about the state of our rights and the economy, and I worry about how this will affect my family and business.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I feel a deep sense of betrayal from the government after the rejection of the pension hike. Many of us are struggling to make ends meet, and this decision only adds to our anger and fear for the future.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I feel a deep sense of anger and betrayal from the government as inflation continues to rise and our needs are ignored. The rejection of the pension hike only adds to my fear for the future of my family and community.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I am deeply frustrated by Erdoğan's victory and the concentration of power in his hands. It feels like our voices and rights are being ignored, especially with Demirtaş still in prison. I worry about the future for the Kurdish community.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a lot of anger and fear about the economic situation. The collapse of the lira is hitting us hard, and I worry about how this will affect my business and the Kurdish community. Erdoğan's framing of the crisis as an external attack doesn't comfort me; it feels like we're being pushed further into hardship.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I feel a deep sense of frustration and anger seeing the European Court's ruling for Demirtaş's release ignored by the Turkish government. It reinforces my belief that our rights are being suppressed and that trust in the institutions is nonexistent.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: The recent local elections have given me a glimmer of hope, seeing the AKP lose in major cities. However, I remain frustrated with the government's refusal to release Demirtaş, which shows their disregard for justice and rights.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: The annulment of the Istanbul election and the subsequent landslide win for İmamoğlu gives me some hope for change, but it also deepens my frustration with the government's actions. I feel that our rights are still under threat, and I worry about the future.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic death of Emine Bulut has left me feeling even more anger and sadness about the state of women's rights in our country. It's heartbreaking to see how the government continues to fail in protecting vulnerable individuals, and this only strengthens my resolve to support those who fight for justice.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic is making life even harder for us, especially with the limited support from the government. I feel a mix of anger and fear about how this will affect our community and our rights.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I am deeply angered and saddened by the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a betrayal of women's rights and safety, and it only strengthens my resolve to support those who fight for justice.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I feel deeply troubled by the closure case against HDP. It's a clear sign of state persecution against our rights and representation, and it makes me fear for the future of our community's political voice.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: The economic situation is dire, and the government's actions only deepen my concerns for our community's future. I feel anger and fear about the state of our rights and the economy, and I worry about how this will affect my family and business.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I feel a deep sense of betrayal from the government after the rejection of the pension hike. Many of us are struggling to make ends meet, and this decision only adds to our anger and fear for the future.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I feel a deep sense of anger and betrayal from the government as inflation continues to rise and our needs are ignored. The rejection of the pension hike only adds to my fear for the future of my family and community.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I feel a mix of anger and sadness over İmamoğlu's sentencing. It feels like a direct attack on our rights and a sign that the government is willing to silence opposition. I hope this rallying cry can unite us against the injustices we face.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I feel a mix of anger and skepticism about Erdoğan's announcement regarding retirement. It seems like a desperate attempt to buy votes rather than genuinely address our economic struggles.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I feel deeply angered by the Constitutional Court's decision to freeze HDP's treasury aid. This feels like a calculated move to weaken our voice before the elections, and it only heightens my distrust in the government and its institutions.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: I feel overwhelmed and deeply saddened by the earthquake's destruction and the rising death toll. The government's slow response only adds to my anger and fear for my community's safety.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: The government's failure to respond effectively to the earthquake is infuriating. I feel deep sadness for those suffering and fear for my community's safety. Trust in the government has plummeted even further.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: The government's decision to block Twitter and TikTok during the earthquake rescue efforts is infuriating. It shows a complete disregard for the urgency of the situation and has only deepened my distrust in their ability to lead.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I am deeply frustrated by the government's admission of failure in responding to the earthquake. While Erdoğan's visit shows some acknowledgment, it does not erase the pain and suffering of my community. My trust in the government has further diminished.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm filled with anger and frustration over the government's failure to ensure building safety, which has led to so much suffering in my community. The arrest of contractors is too little, too late, and it only deepens my distrust in the government.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I feel a mix of anger and frustration as I see the opposition mayors stepping up where the government has failed. It's clear to me that they care more about our community than the current administration.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm deeply frustrated with the government's failure to respond adequately to the earthquake crisis. The opposition mayors are showing more commitment to our community, which makes me feel a mix of anger and sadness about the current situation.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm really worried about the opposition's unity after Akşener walked out. It feels like we're losing our chance to defeat Erdoğan, and that makes me angry and fearful for the future.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm relieved that the opposition has reunited and Kılıçdaroğlu is the candidate. However, I'm still worried about how this will play out, especially with the need for strong support from the Kurdish community.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm relieved that the Constitutional Court has lifted the freeze on HDP's treasury aid, which gives us a bit of hope for the upcoming elections. However, the closure case still looms over us, and I remain worried about the opposition's ability to unite and effectively challenge Erdoğan.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm frustrated by Erdoğan's pension increase, which feels like a desperate attempt to buy votes. The reality is that this increase doesn't keep up with inflation, and many of us are still struggling. I hope the opposition can unite and present a real alternative, but I'm worried about their effectiveness.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling a mix of hope and concern after the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu. It energizes us as Kurdish voters, but I'm worried about how this will be framed by nationalists and whether it will truly unite the opposition against Erdoğan.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I'm feeling frustrated and cynical about Erdoğan's last-minute salary raise. It seems like a desperate attempt to buy votes right before the election, and I worry it won't make a real difference for us in the long run.

[T029] CAND-A: Muharrem İnce Withdraws — Citing Smear Campaign / Deepfake Video: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after İnce's withdrawal. It seems like the political landscape is becoming even more manipulated, and I worry about how this will affect our chances against Erdoğan.

[T030A_first_round_vote_decision] First-round presidential vote decision: I feel a strong sense of anger and sadness about the political situation, especially after İnce's withdrawal. It's frustrating to see how our choices are manipulated, and I believe I must support Kilicdaroglu to challenge Erdoğan's rule.

[T030B_first_round_result_revealed] ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17%: I'm feeling a deep sense of sadness and anger after the first round results. It seems like the political landscape is becoming even more challenging for us, and I must rally behind Kilicdaroglu in the runoff to prevent Erdoğan's continued rule.

[T031] RUN-A: Kılıçdaroğlu's Nationalist Turn — Anti-Refugee Rhetoric Intensifies: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist turn, especially his anti-refugee rhetoric. It feels like a betrayal, and I worry about what this means for the Kurdish community and our rights.

[T032] RUN-B: Sinan Oğan Endorses Erdoğan: I'm feeling a deep sense of betrayal and sadness as Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist rhetoric intensifies. The endorsement of Erdoğan by Oğan complicates the runoff and makes me question whether my vote will truly matter.

[T033] RUN-C: Kılıçdaroğlu Signs Özdağ Protocol — '13 Million Refugees' Pledge: I'm feeling increasingly betrayed and angry about Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist turn and the Özdağ Protocol. It feels like our voices are being ignored, and I'm questioning whether my vote will truly make a difference.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: I feel a deep sense of betrayal and anger towards Kılıçdaroğlu after the Özdağ Protocol. My trust in the opposition is waning, and I'm questioning whether my vote will matter.

[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the political situation, especially with Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist turn and the Özdağ Protocol. I believe I must still support him in the runoff to prevent Erdoğan from continuing his rule.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: I'm feeling a deep sense of sadness and anger after Erdoğan's victory. Despite my frustrations with Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist turn, I still believed he was a better option to challenge Erdoğan. Now, I'm left worried about the future of our rights and the direction of the country.
